title: JIT infrastructure — Cranelift integration and compilation pipeline
purpose: The JIT compiler transforms hot BEAM functions into native machine code via Cranelift. This brief sets up the compilation pipeline: Cranelift dependency, IR builder abstraction, and the tiered execution model (interpret first, compile after N calls).
task: Add Cranelift as an optional dependency, define the JIT compilation pipeline, and implement the hot function detection mechanism.

requirements.title: Cranelift dependency and feature gate
requirements.spec: Add cranelift-codegen and cranelift-frontend as optional dependencies behind a 'jit' feature flag.
acceptance: Cargo.toml has cranelift dependencies behind [features] jit
Feature-gated: jit code compiles only with --features jit

requirements.title: Compilation pipeline scaffold
requirements.spec: Define the JitCompiler struct that takes BEAM bytecode and produces native function pointers.
acceptance: JitCompiler::new() creates a compiler instance with Cranelift settings
NativeCode wraps a function pointer callable from the interpreter
Test: compile a trivial function (return constant)

requirements.title: Hot function detection
requirements.spec: Track function call counts and trigger compilation when a threshold is reached.
acceptance: Per-function call counter (AtomicU32) incremented on call/apply
When threshold reached, function is queued for JIT compilation
Compilation happens on a background thread, not the scheduler thread
Test: call function 1000 times, verify compilation triggered

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any opcode translation in this brief — only the pipeline scaffold
SHALL NOT block the scheduler during compilation — background thread
SHALL be entirely behind the jit feature flag — no runtime cost when disabled
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
